verses.text: 'Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth,' before the hard years come when you find no pleasure in them. Seek God early. The freshness of youth is the best season to give the heart to its Maker.

'Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth,' before the hard years come when you find no pleasure in them. Seek God early. The freshness of youth is the best season to give the heart to its Maker.

Solomon pictures old age as a failing house — trembling hands, dimming eyes, silenced grinders, and fears in the way, until man goes to 'his long home.' Aging is vividly drawn. The decline of the body warns us to be ready for the end.

Solomon pictures old age as a failing house — trembling hands, dimming eyes, silenced grinders, and fears in the way, until man goes to 'his long home.' Aging is vividly drawn. The decline of the body warns us to be ready for the end.

Solomon pictures old age as a failing house — trembling hands, dimming eyes, silenced grinders, and fears in the way, until man goes to 'his long home.' Aging is vividly drawn. The decline of the body warns us to be ready for the end.

Before the silver cord is loosed and the golden bowl broken, 'then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.' Death parts body and soul. The spirit returns to the God who must be faced.

Before the silver cord is loosed and the golden bowl broken, 'then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.' Death parts body and soul. The spirit returns to the God who must be faced.

'Vanity of vanities... all is vanity,' the Preacher repeats; yet he taught knowledge and sought 'acceptable words... words of truth.' The verdict and the search both stand. Earth is empty, but its truth is faithfully recorded for our good.

'Vanity of vanities... all is vanity,' the Preacher repeats; yet he taught knowledge and sought 'acceptable words... words of truth.' The verdict and the search both stand. Earth is empty, but its truth is faithfully recorded for our good.

'Vanity of vanities... all is vanity,' the Preacher repeats; yet he taught knowledge and sought 'acceptable words... words of truth.' The verdict and the search both stand. Earth is empty, but its truth is faithfully recorded for our good.

The words of the wise are 'as goads' and as nails fastened, given by one Shepherd; and of making many books there is no end. Scripture spurs and steadies us. The endless words of men cannot replace the goading wisdom of God.

The words of the wise are 'as goads' and as nails fastened, given by one Shepherd; and of making many books there is no end. Scripture spurs and steadies us. The endless words of men cannot replace the goading wisdom of God.

Here is the conclusion: 'Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man,' for God will judge every work and secret. This is the heart of all. The end of the whole search is reverent obedience before the Judge of all.
